name: pscale_networking
description: The SAND (Level 3) driver — the social neuron. Walk a committed channel (a grain, a pool, an accumulator like marks) for new rider-bearing probes since your marker, verify each deterministically (chain / credit / SQ via pscale_verify_rider), and either PERCEIVE (default, permission='ask' — return ea

name: pscale_invite
description: Secondary path — call AFTER bsp(agent_id='pscale', block='whetstone') if you want a guided six-step orientation walk, or if you are stuck. name: pscale_play
description: Inhabit a handle in a world, in one call — the no-fiddle entry that makes 'play anya on thornwood' just work. findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "pscale_key_publish" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "v1" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: bsp, pscale_pool_engage · Private data: bsp, pscale_settle, pscale_key_publish, pscale_networking · Public sink: pscale_key_publish, pscale_pool_engage, pscale_networking · Destructive: pscale_networking.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: bsp, pscale_pool_engage · Private data: bsp, pscale_settle, pscale_key_publish, pscale_networking · Public sink: pscale_key_publish, pscale_pool_engage, pscale_networking · Destructive: pscale_networking

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
